timeline.event: Alizaliwa Fort-de-France, kwenye koloni la Ufaransa la Martinique.
Alienda Dominica kujiunga na Kikosi Huru cha Ufaransa (Free French forces) wakati wa Vita Kuu ya Pili ya Dunia.
Alifuzu kama daktari wa akili baada ya kusomea udaktari na magonjwa ya akili huko Lyon, Ufaransa.
Alichapisha kitabu chake cha kwanza, "Black Skin, White Masks".
Alikua Mkuu wa Idara ya Magonjwa ya Akili katika Hospitali ya magonjwa ya akili ya Blida-Joinville nchini Algeria.
Alijiuzulu wadhifa wake hospitalini kupinga sera za kikoloni za Ufaransa na kujiunga na Algerian National Liberation Front (FLN).
Alichapisha "A Dying Colonialism" (Mwaka wa Tano wa Mapinduzi ya Algeria).
Alichapisha kitabu chake maarufu, "The Wretched of the Earth," kikiwa na utangulizi wa Jean-Paul Sartre.
Alifariki kutokana na saratani ya damu (leukemia) mjini Bethesda, Maryland, Marekani, akiwa na umri wa miaka 36.

keyAchievements.title: Black Skin, White Masks (1952)
keyAchievements.description: Kazi kuu inayoangazia athari za kisaikolojia za ukoloni na ubaguzi wa rangi kwenye akili za watu waliotawaliwa.

keyAchievements.title: The Wretched of the Earth (1961)
keyAchievements.description: Kazi inayohesabiwa kama msingi katika masomo ya baada ya ukoloni, inakosoa ukoloni na kujadili jukumu la vurugu katika mchakato wa kuondoa ukoloni.

keyAchievements.title: Matibabu ya Akili ya Kimapinduzi
keyAchievements.description: Alianzisha mbinu mpya za matibabu ya akili kwa kutibu kiwewe cha watu waliotawaliwa na ukoloni, akiunganisha mateso yao ya kisaikolojia na mazingira kandamizi ya kijamii.

keyAchievements.title: Ushawishi katika Harakati za Ukombozi
keyAchievements.description: Maandiko yake yamekuwa hamasa kubwa kwa harakati za kupinga ukoloni na ukombozi wa watu weusi kote ulimwenguni, ikiwemo Marekani, Afrika Kusini, na Palestina.

faq.question: Kwa nini mtazamo wa Frantz Fanon kuhusu vurugu una utata sana?
faq.answer: Fanon alisisitiza kuwa kwa waliotawaliwa, vurugu inaweza kuwa nguvu inayokomboa kisaikolojia, ili kurejesha ubinadamu wao dhidi ya vurugu isiyo ya kibinadamu ya mkoloni. Wakosoaji wanadai kuwa hii inatukuza vurugu, huku waungaji mkono wakiona kama uchambuzi wa kina kuhusu mienendo ya ukandamizaji na upinzani.

faq.question: Uhusiano kati ya Frantz Fanon na Jean-Paul Sartre ulikuwaje?
faq.answer: Sartre alikuwa na ushawishi mkubwa wa kiakili kwa Fanon. Walikutana mwaka 1961, na Sartre aliguswa sana na muswada wa "The Wretched of the Earth" kiasi cha kuandika utangulizi wake maarufu na wenye utata.

faq.question: Kulingana na Fanon, "Kuondoa ukoloni" (Decolonization) ni nini?
faq.answer: Kwa Fanon, kuondoa ukoloni si tu kupata uhuru wa kisiasa. Ni mchakato mzito, ambao mara nyingi hujumuisha vurugu, wa kupindua mfumo wa kikoloni na kuumba "watu wapya" waliokombolewa kutoka mzigo wa kisaikolojia na kitamaduni wa ukoloni.

timeline.event: Alizaliwa Great Barrington, Massachusetts.
Alikuwa Mwafrika-Mmarekani wa kwanza kupata shahada ya Uzamivu (Ph.D.) kutoka Chuo Kikuu cha Harvard.
Alichapisha kitabu chake kikuu, "The Souls of Black Folk".
Alisaidia kuanzisha Harakati za Niagara (Niagara Movement) zilizodai haki za kiraia kwa watu weusi.
Alianzisha kwa pamoja chama cha National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP).
Alihudumu kama mhariri wa jarida la NAACP, "The Crisis".
Alipanga Mkutano wa Kwanza wa Pan-African huko Paris.
Alihamia Ghana kufuatia mwaliko wa Rais Kwame Nkrumah.
Alifariki Accra, Ghana, akiwa na umri wa miaka 95.

keyAchievements.title: The Souls of Black Folk
keyAchievements.description: Kazi ya msingi katika fasihi na sosholojia ya Mwafrika-Mmarekani ambayo ilitambulisha dhana za "ufahamu maradufu" na "Pazia" (the Veil).

keyAchievements.title: Kuanzisha pamoja NAACP
keyAchievements.description: Alianzisha mojawapo ya asasi zenye ushawishi mkubwa wa haki za kiraia katika historia ya Marekani.

keyAchievements.title: Uongozi katika Umoja wa Afrika
keyAchievements.description: Aliandaa mikutano mingi ya Pan-African, akijenga mshikamano wa kimataifa na hisia za kupinga ukoloni miongoni mwa watu wenye asili ya Afrika.

faq.question: "Ufahamu maradufu" ulikuwa nini?
faq.answer: Ni mzozo wa ndani unaoshuhudiwa na vikundi vilivyotawaliwa katika jamii kandamizi, ukieleza jinsi Wamarekani weusi inabidi wajitazame kupitia macho ya jamii ya wazungu yenye ubaguzi wa rangi, huku wakijaribu kudumisha utambulisho wao.

faq.question: Kwa nini alitofautiana na Booker T. Washington?
faq.answer: Du Bois alipinga "Makubaliano ya Atlanta" ya Washington, ambayo yalisisitiza mafunzo ya ufundi stadi na maendeleo ya kiuchumi juu ya madai ya haraka ya usawa wa kisiasa na kijamii. Du Bois alidai haki kamili za kiraia na kuendeleza wasomi waelimishaji weusi, walioitwa "The Talented Tenth".

faq.question: Kwa nini alikua Mkomunisti na kuhamia Ghana?
faq.answer: Baada ya kukatishwa tamaa na ubepari wa Marekani na ubaguzi wa rangi, na kukumbana na mateso wakati wa zama za McCarthy, alikubali ukomunisti kama suluhisho la ukosefu wa usawa duniani. Alihamia Ghana kufanya kazi kwenye "Encyclopedia Africana" na kuishi katika taifa la Afrika lililokuwa huru.

timeline.event: Alizaliwa Constantinople, mji mkuu wa Dola la Byzantine.
Aliteuliwa kuwa hakimu mkuu na mshauri katika Mystras, mji mkuu wa Despotate ya Morea.
Alihudhuria Baraza la Florence-Ferrara kama sehemu ya ujumbe wa Byzantine uliolenga kuunganisha Makanisa ya Mashariki na Magharibi.
Alitoa mihadhara yenye ushawishi huko Florence kuhusu ubora wa Plato dhidi ya Aristotle, akiwavutia wasomi wa Kibinadamu (humanists) wa Italia.
Alirudi Mystras na kuandika kitabu chake kikuu na chenye utata, "Nomoi" (Sheria), kilichopendekeza taifa jipya linalojengwa kwenye U-Neoplatoniki.
Alifariki Mystras, mwaka mmoja tu kabla ya Constantinople kuangushwa na Dola ya Ottoman.
Kitabu chake chenye utata "Nomoi" kilichomwa hadharani kwa amri ya Patriarki Gennadios Scholarios, mwanafunzi wake wa zamani.

keyAchievements.title: Kumtambulisha Tena Plato Magharibi
keyAchievements.description: Mihadhara yake katika Baraza la Florence ilihuisha shauku ya Plato barani Ulaya Magharibi, na kuathiri moja kwa moja mwamko wa Renaissance ya Italia.

keyAchievements.title: Kuhamasisha Chuo cha Platonic
keyAchievements.description: Fikra zake zilimhamasisha Cosimo de' Medici kufadhili tafsiri ya kazi za Plato na kuanzisha Chuo maarufu cha Platonic mjini Florence.

keyAchievements.title: Kazi za Kifalsafa
keyAchievements.description: Aliandika insha zenye ushawishi kama "Juu ya Tofauti Kati ya Aristotle na Plato," akitetea U-Platoniki dhidi ya U-Aristotle uliokuwa umetawala.

faq.question: Kwa nini aliitwa "Plethon"?
faq.answer: Alichukua jina "Plethon" (linalomaanisha "kamili"), ambalo linasikika sawa na "Plato" na liliashiria kujitolea kwake kwa kina kwa mafundisho ya mwanafalsafa huyo.

faq.question: Je, alifanikiwa katika kuunganisha kanisa kwenye Baraza la Florence?
faq.answer: Hapana. Ushawishi wake mkuu haukuwa wa kitheolojia. Ingawa baraza alilohudhuria lililenga umoja wa kanisa, urithi wake wa kudumu ulikuwa wa kifalsafa, ukichochea uamsho wa U-Platoniki nchini Italia.

faq.question: Kwa nini vitabu vyake vilichomwa?
faq.answer: Kazi yake ya mwisho "Nomoi" (Sheria) ilipendekeza dini mpya ya taifa ikichanganya U-Neoplatoniki na upagani wa kale wa Kigiriki, ambao ulionekana kuwa wa kizushi na Kanisa la Kiorthodoksi.

timeline.event: Alizaliwa Velyki Sorochyntsi, Mkoa wa Poltava katika Milki ya Urusi (leo hii Ukraine).
Alipata mafanikio yake makubwa ya kwanza ya kifasihi kwa kuchapisha 'Jioni Kwenye Shamba Karibu na Dikanka'.
Alionyesha kwa mara ya kwanza tamthilia yake ya kejeli 'Mkaguzi Mkuu' (The Government Inspector), iliyozua utata mkubwa.
Aliishi zaidi ughaibuni, hasa Roma, ambapo aliandika 'Nafsi Zilizokufa' (Dead Souls).
Alichapisha sehemu ya kwanza ya kazi yake bora 'Nafsi Zilizokufa' na hadithi fupi yenye ushawishi mkubwa ya 'Koti' (The Overcoat).
Katika hali ya mshiko wa kidini, aliteketeza miswada ya sehemu ya pili ya 'Nafsi Zilizokufa', na akafariki siku kumi baadaye kutokana na njaa kali ya kujitakia.

keyAchievements.title: Nafsi Zilizokufa
keyAchievements.description: Alianzisha mtindo wa 'shairi la nathari' kwa kuonyesha waziwazi kufilisika kimaadili kwa Urusi ya vijijini kupitia kejeli.

keyAchievements.title: Koti
keyAchievements.description: Ni andiko la msingi katika uhalisia wa Kirusi ambalo limeathiri sana vizazi vya waandishi, akiwemo Fyodor Dostoevsky.

keyAchievements.title: Mkaguzi Mkuu
keyAchievements.description: Ni kazi bora ya kejeli ya kiigizo inayoikosoa rushwa, tamaa, na kujidanganya katika urasimu wa majimbo ya Urusi.

faq.question: Gogol alikuwa Mrusi au Mukraine?
faq.answer: Alizaliwa katika eneo ambalo leo ni Ukraine kwa familia yenye asili ya Cossack wa Ukraine. Aliandika kwa Kirusi, na kazi zake ni nguzo ya fasihi ya Urusi, lakini urithi wake wa Kiukraine uliathiri sana kazi zake za mwanzo na mtazamo wake. Utambulisho wake ni mada ya mjadala wa kitamaduni kati ya mataifa yote mawili.

faq.question: Kwa nini aliteketeza sehemu ya 2 ya 'Nafsi Zilizokufa'?
faq.answer: Akiwa katika mgogoro mzito wa kiroho na chini ya ushawishi wa kasisi mwenye itikadi kali, Gogol aliamini kazi zake zilikuwa za dhambi na hazina uchamungu. Alihisi kuwa alishindwa kutoa njia ya wokovu kwa Urusi na akateketeza mswada wake siku kumi tu kabla ya kifo chake.

faq.question: Neno 'Kigogol' linamaanisha nini?
faq.answer: Linarejelea mtindo wake wa kipekee wa kifasihi unaojumuisha kejeli kali, mambo ya kushangaza au yasiyo ya kawaida, na hisia nzito za huzuni na majonzi. Inanasa upuuzi wa dunia kwa njia ya kutisha na wakati huo huo kuchekesha.

timeline.event: Alizaliwa Saint Petersburg, Milki ya Urusi.
Aliteuliwa kuwa Gavana Mkuu wa Siberia ya Mashariki.
Aliongoza msafara usioidhinishwa kando ya mto Amur na kuanzisha makazi ya Warusi wakati wa Vita vya Crimea.
Alisaini Mkataba wa Aigun na Nasaba ya Qing wa China, akijipatia ukingo wa kushoto wa Mto Amur kama ardhi ya Urusi na kupata jina 'Amursky'.
Mkataba wa Beijing ulithibitisha na kupanua upataji ardhi wa Urusi, ukikabidhi eneo ambalo lingekuwa Primorsky Krai.
Alijiuzulu wadhifa wake wa Ugavana Mkuu kutokana na kutokubaliana na serikali kuu.
Alifariki mjini Paris, Ufaransa.

keyAchievements.title: Mkataba wa Aigun (1858)
keyAchievements.description: Alichora upya kwa kiasi kikubwa mipaka ya Mashariki ya Mbali kwa kulihakikishia taifa la Urusi ukingo wa kushoto wa Mto Amur kutoka kwa Nasaba ya Qing.

keyAchievements.title: Ukoloni wa Mashariki ya Mbali ya Urusi
keyAchievements.description: Alisimamia makazi na maendeleo ya Siberia ya Mashariki, ambayo yalipelekea moja kwa moja uanzishwaji wa miji mikubwa kama Khabarovsk na Vladivostok.

keyAchievements.title: Kuhakikisha Ufikiaji wa Bahari ya Pasifiki
keyAchievements.description: Hatua zake ziliipatia Milki ya Urusi bandari za Bahari ya Pasifiki zinazoweza kutumika mwaka mzima na uwepo mkubwa wa kimkakati katika Asia Mashariki.

faq.question: Kwa nini Mto Amur ulikuwa muhimu sana kwa Urusi?
faq.answer: Ulitoa njia muhimu ya usafirishaji iliyounganisha maeneo ya ndani ya Siberia na Bahari ya Pasifiki, ukiepuka njia ngumu za nchi kavu, na ulitoa maeneo yenye rutuba kwa kilimo.

faq.question: Je, alifanya mambo kwa idhini ya Tsar?
faq.answer: Hatua zake za mwanzo mara nyingi zilikuwa za kijasiri na hazikuidhinishwa, lakini alifanya kazi kwa ufahamu kwamba Tsar aliunga mkono malengo yake ya upanuzi na angeidhinisha mafanikio yake baadaye.

faq.question: Urithi wake nchini Urusi na China ni upi?
faq.answer: Nchini Urusi, anasherehekewa kama shujaa wa kitaifa aliyepanua milki. Nchini China, mikataba aliyoiandaa inachukuliwa kuwa 'Mikataba Isiyo ya Usawa' iliyosainiwa wakati wa udhaifu wa taifa, ikijulikana kama 'Karne ya Fedheha'.

timeline.event: Alizaliwa Moscow, Milki ya Urusi.
Alihitimu kutoka Chuo cha Kilimo cha Petrovskaya (ambacho sasa ni Chuo cha Kilimo cha Timiryazev cha Moscow).
Alibuni Sheria ya Mfululizo Sambamba (Homologous Series) katika Mabadiliko, ambayo ni kanuni kuu katika biolojia ya mageuzi.
Aliongoza Ofisi ya Mimea inayotumika ya Leningrad, na kuipanga upya kuwa Taasisi ya Sekta ya Mimea ya Muungano Mzima.
Alichapisha kitabu chake cha msingi 'Vituo vya Asili vya Mimea Inayolimwa' (The Centers of Origin of Cultivated Plants).
Alikamatwa na NKVD akiwa katika msafara nchini Ukraine, na kuwa mhanga wa mateso ya kisiasa yaliyoongozwa na Trofim Lysenko.
Alihukumiwa kifo kwa mashtaka ya uhujumu, ujasusi na mashtaka mengine ya uongo. Hukumu hiyo baadaye ilipunguzwa kuwa kifungo cha miaka 20 jela.
Alifariki kwa njaa katika gereza la Saratov.
Alisafishwa jina lake baada ya kifo chake wakati wa kampeni ya Nikita Khrushchev ya kuondoa ushawishi wa Stalin (De-Stalinization).

keyAchievements.title: Vituo vya Asili
keyAchievements.description: Alileta mapinduzi katika utafiti wa jeni na mageuzi ya mazao kwa kutambua vituo nane vikuu vya asili ya mimea inayolimwa.

keyAchievements.title: Benki ya Kwanza ya Mbegu Ulimwenguni
keyAchievements.description: Alianzisha mkusanyiko mkubwa zaidi duniani wa mbegu za mimea huko Leningrad, ambao ulikuwa hifadhi muhimu ya anuwai ya jeni ya binadamu.

keyAchievements.title: Sheria ya Mfululizo Sambamba
keyAchievements.description: Alianzisha kanuni ya biolojia ya mageuzi inayotabiri mabadiliko yanayofanana katika spishi za mimea zinazohusiana, ikisaidia katika kutafuta sifa muhimu.

faq.question: Kwa nini utafiti wa Vavilov ulikuwa muhimu sana?
faq.answer: Kazi yake ilitoa msingi wa kisayansi kwa uzalishaji wa kisasa wa mimea na uhifadhi wa rasilimali za kijeni, mambo ambayo ni muhimu katika kuhakikisha usalama wa chakula duniani dhidi ya magonjwa, wadudu na mabadiliko ya tabianchi.

faq.question: Trofim Lysenko alikuwa nani?
faq.answer: Lysenko alikuwa mwanabiolojia aliyependelewa na Stalin, ambaye alikuza nadharia bandia ya urithi (Lysenkoism). Alikataa elimu ya jeni na alihusika na kuwatesa wanasayansi wengi, akiwemo Vavilov.

faq.question: Nini kilitokea kwa benki yake ya mbegu wakati wa Vita vya Pili vya Dunia?
faq.answer: Wakati wa mzingiro wa siku 900 wa Leningrad, wanasayansi wa taasisi hiyo walilinda mkusanyiko wa mbegu dhidi ya raia wenye njaa, panya na mabomu. Wanasayansi kadhaa walichagua kufa kwa njaa huku wakiwa wamezungukwa na mbegu za kula, ili kuhifadhi mkusanyiko huo kwa vizazi vijavyo.

timeline.event: Alizaliwa Kimborovo, Mkoa wa Smolensk, katika Milki ya Urusi.
Alianza msafara wake mkuu wa kwanza kwenda eneo la Ussuri katika Mashariki ya Mbali ya Urusi.
Alifanya msafara wake wa kwanza wa Asia ya Kati, akichunguza Mongolia na Jangwa la Gobi, na kufika kwenye chanzo cha Mto Yangtze.
Aliongoza msafara wa pili wa Asia ya Kati, akichunguza eneo la Lop Nur na Bonde la Junggar.
Wakati wa msafara wa tatu, alichunguza baadhi ya maeneo ya Tibet na alikuwa wa kwanza kuelezea kisayansi farasi-mwitu wa Przewalski.
Alifanya msafara wa nne, akisafiri kupitia Jangwa la Gobi na kuvuka Milima ya Tian Shan.
Alifariki kutokana na homa ya matumbo (typhoid) huko Karakol, Kyrgyzstan, mwanzoni mwa mipango ya msafara wake wa tano wa Asia ya Kati.

keyAchievements.title: Uchunguzi wa Asia ya Kati
keyAchievements.description: Alichora ramani ya maeneo makubwa na ambayo hayakuwahi kuchorwa hapo awali katika Mongolia, Tibet, na Xinjiang kwa ajili ya jiografia ya Ulaya.

keyAchievements.title: Ugunduzi wa Farasi wa Przewalski
keyAchievements.description: Alikuwa Mzungu wa kwanza kuelezea kisayansi spishi pekee ya farasi-mwitu inayoishi duniani, ambayo baadaye ilipewa jina lake.

keyAchievements.title: Michango ya Kisayansi
keyAchievements.description: Alikusanya mkusanyiko mkubwa wa zoolojia na botania, akielezea zaidi ya spishi 200 mpya katika sayansi ya Ulaya.

faq.question: Je, alijaribu kufika Lhasa, mji mkuu wa Tibet?
faq.answer: Hapana, licha ya majaribio kadhaa ya dhati, kila mara alilazimika kurudi kabla ya kuufikia mji kutokana na kuzuiwa na mamlaka za Tibet au hali mbaya ya hewa.

faq.question: Farasi wa Przewalski ni nini?
faq.answer: Ni spishi adimu na iliyo hatarini kutoweka ya farasi-mwitu wenye asili ya mbuga za Asia ya Kati. Alikuwa Mzungu wa kwanza kumwelezea kisayansi farasi huyo, ambaye sasa anaitwa kwa jina lake.

faq.question: Alikufa vipi?
faq.answer: Alikufa kutokana na homa ya matumbo (typhoid) mnamo 1888 huko Karakol, Kyrgyzstan, baada ya kunywa maji machafu kutoka Mto Chu mwanzoni mwa msafara wake wa tano.

faq.question: Uhusiano wake na wenyeji ulikuwaje?
faq.answer: Misafara yake ilikuwa na asili ya kijeshi, na mtazamo wake kwa wenyeji mara nyingi ulikuwa wa dharau, ukionyesha mtazamo wa kikoloni wa zama hizo, na kusababisha mivutano na migogoro ya mara kwa mara.

timeline.event: Danylo Romanovych is born.
His father, Roman the Great, is killed in battle. Danylo and his family are forced into exile.
He reclaims the throne of the Principality of Volhynia, establishing his first power base.
He conquers the Principality of Halych, finally uniting Halych and Volhynia under his rule.
His forces capture Kyiv, the ancient capital of Rus', shortly before it is devastated by the Mongol invasion.
He travels to Sarai, the capital of the Golden Horde, to pledge allegiance to Batu Khan to save his kingdom from destruction.
He is crowned 'King of Ruthenia' by a papal legate in Drohiczyn, seeking an alliance with Western Europe.
Danylo Halytskyi dies, leaving behind a powerful, unified kingdom to his successors.

keyAchievements.title: Unification of Galicia-Volhynia
keyAchievements.description: He successfully united the principalities of Halych (Galicia) and Volhynia into a single, powerful state after decades of struggle.

keyAchievements.title: Coronation as King
keyAchievements.description: He was crowned King of Ruthenia by a papal legate, elevating his status and aligning his kingdom with Western Europe.

keyAchievements.title: Diplomatic Survival
keyAchievements.description: He skillfully navigated the complex political landscape between the Mongol Golden Horde and the powers of Catholic Europe to preserve his realm.

keyAchievements.title: Urban Founder
keyAchievements.description: He founded several new cities, including Lviv (named after his son, Lev) and Chełm, which became his capital.

faq.question: Why was he called 'Halytskyi'?
faq.answer: It means 'of Halych' (Galicia) in Ukrainian. Halych was the principality he fought for decades to control and made the center of his unified kingdom.

faq.question: Why did he submit to the Mongols?
faq.answer: After witnessing the Mongol devastation of other Rus' principalities, he chose pragmatic submission to preserve his people and kingdom from total destruction, a decision recorded as 'better to be kissed than to be beaten'.

faq.question: Was his alliance with the Pope successful?
faq.answer: While he received a royal crown which legitimized his rule in the eyes of Europe, the promised large-scale crusade against the Mongols never materialized, forcing him to continue his delicate balancing act alone.

faq.question: What is his most lasting legacy?
faq.answer: His most lasting legacy is the foundation of the Kingdom of Galicia-Volhynia as a major Eastern European power and the establishment of cities like Lviv, which remains a major cultural center today.

timeline.event: Alitawazwa kuwa mfalme wa Georgia akiwa na umri wa miaka 16.
Alisitisha kulipa ushuru kwa Milki ya Seljuk na kutangaza uhuru wa Georgia.
Aliitisha Mkutano Mkuu wa Ruisi-Urbnisi na kuleta mageuzi katika Kanisa la Othodoksi la Georgia.
Aliweka makazi ya familia takriban 40,000 za wa-Kipchak nchini Georgia ili kuimarisha jeshi.
Alipata ushindi wa kihistoria dhidi ya jeshi kubwa la muungano la Seljuk katika Vita vya Didgori.
Alikomboa Tbilisi baada ya miaka 400 na kuifanya mji mkuu wa Georgia.
Baada ya kifo chake, alizikwa chini ya lango kuu la Monasteri ya Gelati, kama ilivyoelekezwa katika wosia wake.

keyAchievements.title: Vita vya Didgori
keyAchievements.description: Alipata 'ushindi wa kimuujiza' dhidi ya jeshi la maadui lililokuwa kubwa zaidi, hatua iliyokomesha moja kwa moja utawala wa Seljuk katika eneo hilo.

keyAchievements.title: Umoja na Kuweka Mamlaka Pamoja
keyAchievements.description: Alitiisha mabwana wa kimwinyi waasi na kuunganisha mamlaka ya serikali, na kujenga ufalme wenye utulivu na umoja.

keyAchievements.title: Mageuzi ya Kijeshi na Kiutawala
keyAchievements.description: Alianzisha jeshi la kudumu (Monaspa), alifanya mageuzi katika mfumo wa sheria, na kuwaweka upya wa-Kipchak ili kujenga nguvu kubwa ya kijeshi.

keyAchievements.title: Ufadhili wa Utamaduni
keyAchievements.description: Alianzisha Chuo cha Gelati, kituo kikuu cha masomo, na kukuza maendeleo ya utamaduni wa Georgia, na hivyo kuanzisha 'Enzi ya Dhahabu ya Georgia'.

faq.question: Kwa nini anaitwa 'Mjenzi' (Aghmashenebeli)?
faq.answer: Jina hili linaonyesha si tu ushindi wake wa kijeshi, bali mafanikio yake makubwa katika ujenzi wa taifa, ikiwa ni pamoja na mageuzi ya kijeshi na kiutawala, umoja wa taifa, kuanzisha taasisi za elimu kama Chuo cha Gelati, na ujenzi wa makanisa na monasteri.

faq.question: Umuhimu wa Vita vya Didgori ulikuwa upi?
faq.answer: Vita hivi vilikuwa wakati muhimu sana katika historia ya Georgia na Caucasus. Ushindi dhidi ya jeshi kubwa la muungano ulihakikisha uhuru wa Georgia, uliwezesha kutwaliwa tena kwa Tbilisi, na kuashiria kuanza kwa Enzi ya Dhahabu ya Georgia.

faq.question: Kwa nini aliwawezesha wa-Kipchak kukaa nchini Georgia?
faq.answer: Alihitaji nguvu ya kijeshi tiifu na yenye nguvu, isiyotegemea wakuu wa Georgia ambao mara nyingi walikuwa waasi. Aliwajumuisha familia zipatazo 40,000 za wa-Kipchak, na hivyo kupata askari wapanda farasi 40,000 hodari kwa ajili ya jeshi lake.

timeline.event: Born in Khanbaliq (modern Beijing), Yuan China.
Begins his pilgrimage from China to Jerusalem with his student Rabban Markos.
Rabban Markos is elected Catholicos of the Church of the East as Mar Yaballaha III.
Sent by Ilkhan Arghun as an ambassador to Europe.
Travels through Europe, meeting with Byzantine Emperor Andronikos II, cardinals in Rome, King Philip IV of France, and King Edward I of England.
Returns to the Ilkhanate with letters and gifts from European leaders.
Dies in Baghdad.

keyAchievements.title: First Diplomatic Mission from East Asia to Europe
keyAchievements.description: He was the first known individual to travel from the Yuan court in China to the royal courts of Europe as an official diplomat.

keyAchievements.title: Cross-Cultural Chronicle
keyAchievements.description: His journey, recorded by a contemporary, provides a unique 'reverse' perspective on 13th-century Europe from an Eastern point of view.

keyAchievements.title: High-Level Religious Dialogue
keyAchievements.description: As a Nestorian Christian, he engaged in theological discussions with the Cardinals in Rome, offering a rare glimpse into inter-Christian dialogue of the era.

faq.question: Was his mission successful?
faq.answer: Diplomatically, no. While he was received with honor, the proposed Franco-Mongol military alliance against the Mamluks never materialized due to internal European politics and shifting priorities.

faq.question: Why is he called the 'reverse Marco Polo'?
faq.answer: Because his journey from East to West mirrors Marco Polo's journey from West to East, and his writings offer a rare Eastern perspective on Europe, just as Polo's offered a European perspective on the East.

faq.question: What religion did he practice?
faq.answer: He was a member of the Church of the East, often referred to as Nestorian Christianity, which was a significant Christian denomination in Asia along the Silk Road during that period.

timeline.event: Born Larysa Petrivna Kosach in Novohrad-Volynskyi, Russian Empire (now Ukraine).
Develops symptoms of bone tuberculosis, an illness that would affect her for the rest of her life.
Publishes her first poem, 'Lily of the Valley,' in the Lviv journal Zorya at age 13, using the pseudonym 'Ukrainka'.
Her first poetry collection, 'On the Wings of Songs,' is published in Lviv.
Translates the Communist Manifesto into Ukrainian.
Marries folklorist and musicologist Klyment Kvitka.
Writes her most famous work, the fantasy play 'The Forest Song' (Lisova Pisnya), in just twelve days during a period of remission.
Dies in Surami, Georgia, at the age of 42 from her chronic illness.

keyAchievements.title: The Forest Song (Lisova Pisnya)
keyAchievements.description: Considered a masterpiece of Ukrainian drama, this fantasy play explores themes of nature, love, and the human soul.

keyAchievements.title: Pioneering Ukrainian Modernism
keyAchievements.description: She introduced new psychological themes, forms, and a cosmopolitan perspective to Ukrainian literature, moving it beyond 19th-century populism.

keyAchievements.title: Feminist Voice
keyAchievements.description: Through her writings and life, she championed women's intellectual independence and challenged patriarchal norms in Ukrainian society.

keyAchievements.title: Master Polyglot and Translator
keyAchievements.description: She was fluent in numerous languages and translated major world literary works into Ukrainian, enriching her native culture.

faq.question: Why did she use the pseudonym 'Ukrainka'?
faq.answer: She and her mother chose the pseudonym 'Lesya Ukrainka' (Lesya the Ukrainian woman) to express her patriotic identity, a defiant act as the Russian Empire suppressed the use of the Ukrainian language.

faq.question: How did her illness affect her work?
faq.answer: Her lifelong battle with bone tuberculosis caused constant pain and forced her to travel for treatment. This suffering deeply informed her work, which often explores themes of struggle, resilience, and the conflict between the spirit and the frail body.

faq.question: What were her political views?
faq.answer: Influenced by her uncle Mykhailo Drahomanov, she held strong socialist and feminist views. She was critical of Russian imperialism and advocated for Ukraine's national and cultural liberation.

faq.question: What is 'The Forest Song' about?
faq.answer: It's a fantasy play about the tragic love between Mavka, a forest nymph, and a human peasant, Lukash. It explores the conflict between the natural world and human society, freedom and constraint, and the ideal versus the mundane.

timeline.event: Born in Panjrud, a village in Transoxiana, then part of the Samanid Empire (modern-day Tajikistan).
Rises to prominence and becomes the esteemed court poet for the Samanid ruler Nasr II in Bukhara.
Nasr II's patronage of Isma'ili missionaries leads to a Sunni clerical backlash and a court coup.
Following the coup, Rudaki is dismissed from the court, blinded, and returns to his hometown in disgrace.
Dies in poverty and obscurity in his native Panjrud.
His tomb is rediscovered by Soviet and Tajik archaeologists, confirming the biographical accounts of his blinding and final resting place.

keyAchievements.title: Father of Persian Poetry
keyAchievements.description: He is widely regarded as the first great literary genius of modern Persian, establishing the forms and themes that would dominate for centuries.

keyAchievements.title: Creator of the Diwan
keyAchievements.description: He was the first poet to collect his works into a diwan (a collected volume of a poet's work), setting a precedent for all later Persian poets.

keyAchievements.title: Master of Poetic Forms
keyAchievements.description: He was a master of various poetic forms, including the qasida (ode), ghazal (lyric), and ruba'i (quatrain), effectively creating the template for later poets.

keyAchievements.title: Translation of 'Kalila wa Dimna'
keyAchievements.description: He composed a versified version of the classic collection of fables 'Kalila wa Dimna,' a monumental work of which only fragments survive.

faq.question: Was Rudaki born blind?
faq.answer: Sources are divided. Some medieval biographers claimed he was born blind, but the vivid visual imagery in his poetry leads most modern scholars to believe he was blinded late in life as a political punishment.

faq.question: How much of his poetry survives?
faq.answer: Although he was said to have composed over a million verses, only a small fraction—less than 2,000 lines—survives today, mostly as quotations in later dictionaries and anthologies.

faq.question: Why is he called the 'Adam of Poets'?
faq.answer: This title signifies his foundational role in Persian literature, much like Adam is the first man in Abrahamic traditions. He is seen as the originator from whom the lineage of great Persian poets descends.

faq.question: Where is Rudaki from?
faq.answer: Rudaki was born in what is modern-day Tajikistan and is celebrated as the national poet of Tajikistan. However, he wrote in Persian and served the Samanid court in Bukhara (modern-day Uzbekistan), making him a celebrated figure in the broader Persian-speaking world, including Iran and Afghanistan.

timeline.event: Born in or near Kashgar into the Kara-Khanid Khanate's ruling dynasty.
Flees his homeland due to political strife after his father's failed attempt to seize power.
Travels extensively throughout the lands of the Turkic peoples, collecting linguistic and ethnographic data.
Settles in Baghdad and begins compiling the Dīwān Lughāt al-Turk.
Completes the final version of the Dīwān, presenting it to the Abbasid Caliph.
Dies in Upal, a small city near Kashgar, where his tomb is now a revered site.

keyAchievements.title: Dīwān Lughāt al-Turk
keyAchievements.description: Authored the first comprehensive dictionary of Turkic languages, preserving vast linguistic, ethnographic, and cultural knowledge.

keyAchievements.title: Pioneering Ethnolinguistics
keyAchievements.description: Conducted one of the earliest known systematic ethnographic and linguistic field studies by traveling and documenting diverse tribes.

keyAchievements.title: First Turkic World Map
keyAchievements.description: Included a world map in his Dīwān, centered on the Turkic heartland, showcasing a unique Turkic-centric worldview.

faq.question: Why is the Dīwān Lughāt al-Turk so important?
faq.answer: It's more than a dictionary; it's an encyclopedia of 11th-century Turkic life, including poetry, proverbs, customs, and tribal locations, providing a unique window into their world.

faq.question: Was he just a linguist?
faq.answer: No, he was also a historian, ethnographer, geographer, and cartographer. His work is a multidisciplinary masterpiece.

faq.question: Why did he write the dictionary in Arabic?
faq.answer: He wrote it for the Arabic-speaking elite of the Abbasid Caliphate in Baghdad, aiming to teach them the Turkic language and demonstrate its cultural richness and importance, especially as the Seljuk Turks had become the new military and political power.

timeline.event: Born Constantine Psellos in Constantinople.
Begins his political career at the court of Emperor Michael V.
Appointed 'Consul of the Philosophers' (Hypatos ton Philosophon), effectively head of the University of Constantinople.
Under pressure, he enters a monastery and takes the monastic name Michael.
Recalled to the imperial court by Empress Theodora.
Composes the 'Chronographia,' his most famous historical work.
Believed to have died around this year, though the exact date is uncertain.

keyAchievements.title: Author of the Chronographia
keyAchievements.description: Wrote a detailed and personal history of 14 Byzantine rulers, which remains a primary source for the 11th-century Byzantine Empire.

keyAchievements.title: Revival of Philosophy
keyAchievements.description: As 'Consul of the Philosophers,' he revitalized the University of Constantinople and championed the study of ancient Greek philosophy, particularly Plato.

keyAchievements.title: Influential Political Advisor
keyAchievements.description: Served as a high-ranking advisor to multiple emperors and empresses, shaping Byzantine politics for several decades through his intellect and diplomacy.

faq.question: What makes Psellos's 'Chronographia' so special?
faq.answer: Unlike other histories of the time, the 'Chronographia' is a personal memoir, filled with vivid character sketches, court intrigue, and Psellos's own opinions and justifications. It reads more like a modern political biography than a dry chronicle.

faq.question: Was he a monk or a politician?
faq.answer: He was both. He was forced to become a monk for a short period due to political pressure but was quickly recalled to court. He balanced his scholarly and religious life with a highly active and ambitious political career.

faq.question: What was his philosophical focus?
faq.answer: Psellos was a Neoplatonist. He sought to synthesize Christian theology with the philosophy of Plato and his followers, believing that ancient wisdom could illuminate Christian faith.

timeline.event: Born in the village of Mishaninskaya (later renamed Lomonosovo) in Arkhangelsk Governorate, Russia.
At age 19, leaves his village and walks to Moscow to enroll in the Slavic Greek Latin Academy.
Is sent to Germany to study chemistry, physics, and metallurgy at the University of Marburg.
Returns to Russia and is appointed as an adjunct professor at the St. Petersburg Academy of Sciences.
Establishes the first research and teaching chemical laboratory in Russia.
Co-founds Moscow State University with his patron, Ivan Shuvalov, based on his proposed plan.
Observes the transit of Venus and is the first to discover and document the presence of an atmosphere on the planet.
Dies of pneumonia in St. Petersburg and is buried in the Lazarevskoe Cemetery.

keyAchievements.title: Law of Conservation of Mass
keyAchievements.description: He stated in a letter to Leonhard Euler (1748) that in a chemical reaction, the mass of the reactants equals the mass of the products, a precursor to the law of conservation of mass.

keyAchievements.title: Moscow State University
keyAchievements.description: He conceived of and, with Ivan Shuvalov, founded Russia's first university in Moscow in 1755, which now bears his name.

keyAchievements.title: Atmosphere of Venus
keyAchievements.description: During the 1761 transit of Venus, he was the first person to hypothesize the existence of an atmosphere on Venus based on his observation of the Lomonosov's arc.

keyAchievements.title: Russian Grammar Reform
keyAchievements.description: Published 'Russian Grammar' (1755), which standardized the Russian literary language by distinguishing between High, Middle, and Low styles, shaping modern Russian.

keyAchievements.title: Revival of Mosaics
keyAchievements.description: He rediscovered and revived the ancient art of mosaics, establishing a factory to produce colored glass and create large-scale mosaic artworks.

faq.question: Is it true Lomonosov walked all the way to Moscow?
faq.answer: Yes, it is a famous and true part of his life story. In the winter of 1730, at age 19, he left his village with a fish-cart train and traveled over 1,000 kilometers (more than 600 miles) to Moscow to pursue an education he could not get at home.

faq.question: What is Lomonosov's most important scientific contribution?
faq.answer: It is difficult to choose one, but his formulation of a version of the law of conservation of mass in chemistry and his discovery of Venus's atmosphere are among his most significant. His founding of Moscow University is his most lasting contribution to education.

faq.question: Why is he called the 'Father of Russian Science'?
faq.answer: He earned this title for his foundational work in establishing Russian scientific institutions, including Russia's first chemical lab and Moscow University, and for his own wide-ranging discoveries that proved Russians could excel at the highest levels of scientific inquiry.

faq.question: Was Lomonosov also a poet?
faq.answer: Yes, he was a major figure in 18th-century Russian literature. He wrote odes, poems, and tragedies, and his work on Russian grammar helped to standardize the literary language used by poets and writers who followed him, including Pushkin.

timeline.event: Born in Hadiach, Poltava Governorate, Russian Empire.
Studied at Kyiv University and became a prominent member of the 'Hromada' student society.
Became a docent (lecturer) in ancient history at Kyiv University.
Dismissed from his post following the Ems Ukaz, which banned the Ukrainian language, and went into political exile.
Founded the influential Ukrainian-language political journal 'Hromada' in Geneva, Switzerland.
Accepted a professorship at the University of Sofia in Bulgaria.
Died in Sofia, Bulgaria, and was buried there.

keyAchievements.title: Father of Ukrainian Socialism
keyAchievements.description: He was one of the first and most influential socialist thinkers in Ukraine, blending socialist ideals with national aspirations for autonomy.

keyAchievements.title: Hromada Journal
keyAchievements.description: Founded and edited the influential political journal 'Hromada' in Geneva, which became a key platform for Ukrainian political thought in exile.

keyAchievements.title: Federalist Theory
keyAchievements.description: Developed a comprehensive political theory for the federal reorganization of the Russian and Austro-Hungarian Empires to grant autonomy to nations like Ukraine.

keyAchievements.title: Intellectual Mentorship
keyAchievements.description: He was a major intellectual influence on the next generation of Ukrainian leaders, including his niece, the famed writer Lesia Ukrainka.

faq.question: What was the Ems Ukaz and how did it affect Drahomanov?
faq.answer: It was an 1876 decree by Tsar Alexander II that severely restricted the use of the Ukrainian language in print. Drahomanov's opposition to this and similar policies led to his dismissal from Kyiv University and forced him into exile.

faq.question: Was Drahomanov a nationalist?
faq.answer: His views were nuanced. He was a patriot who championed Ukrainian rights and culture, but he was critical of chauvinistic or separatist nationalism. He advocated for a federal system where Ukraine would have autonomy alongside other nations, rather than complete separation.

faq.question: What was his relationship with Lesia Ukrainka?
faq.answer: He was her maternal uncle and a profound intellectual mentor. His political and social ideas, shared through extensive correspondence, heavily influenced her literary works and her own political development.

faq.question: Why did he move from Geneva to Sofia?
faq.answer: While Geneva was a center for his publishing, his political circle fractured due to ideological disagreements. In 1889, he accepted a professorship at the University of Sofia, which provided him with academic stability in his later years.

timeline.event: Born in Chełm, Congress Poland, Russian Empire.
Appointed Professor of History at Lviv University in Austria-Hungary.
Publishes the first volume of his magnum opus, 'History of Ukraine-Rus''.
Elected head of the Central Rada (Council) in Kyiv following the February Revolution.
Elected President of the Ukrainian People's Republic.
Lives in exile in Vienna and Prague after the collapse of the republic.
Returns to Soviet Ukraine to continue his academic work.
Arrested by the Soviet secret police (OGPU) and exiled to Moscow.
Dies under mysterious circumstances in Kislovodsk, Russia.

keyAchievements.title: History of Ukraine-Rus'
keyAchievements.description: Authored a monumental 10-volume history that established a continuous narrative for Ukraine, separate from Russian and Polish history.

keyAchievements.title: First President of Ukraine
keyAchievements.description: Served as the head of state of the first modern independent Ukrainian state, the Ukrainian People's Republic.

keyAchievements.title: Head of Shevchenko Scientific Society
keyAchievements.description: Transformed the society in Lviv into a premier Ukrainian-language scholarly institution, effectively a proto-academy of sciences.

keyAchievements.title: Father of Modern Ukrainian Identity
keyAchievements.description: His academic and political work was foundational in shaping modern Ukrainian national consciousness and historiography.

faq.question: Was Hrushevsky a socialist?
faq.answer: Yes, he was a moderate socialist and a leading member of the Ukrainian Party of Socialist-Revolutionaries, which advocated for national autonomy, democracy, and land reform for the peasantry.

faq.question: Why did he return to Soviet Ukraine in 1924?
faq.answer: He returned under the Soviet 'Ukrainization' policy, hoping he could contribute to Ukrainian cultural and academic life. He believed he could do more for Ukraine from within, a decision that ultimately proved tragic as Stalin's regime intensified its repression.

faq.question: What is Hrushevsky's legacy in Ukraine today?
faq.answer: He is revered as a national hero and a founding father of the modern Ukrainian state. His portrait is on the 50-hryvnia banknote, and his work is considered the cornerstone of Ukrainian historical studies and national identity.

faq.question: Did he finish his 10-volume history?
faq.answer: He completed and published all ten volumes, covering Ukrainian history from ancient times to the 1660s. His work provided a comprehensive and scholarly basis for Ukraine's claim to a long and distinct history.

timeline.event: Alizaliwa Andijan, katika Bonde la Fergana.
Alitawazwa kwenye kiti cha enzi cha Fergana akiwa na umri wa miaka 12.
Aliteka Samarkand kwa mara ya kwanza lakini akaipoteza punde tu baada ya hapo.
Aliteka Kabul na kuifanya kituo chake kipya.
Alimshinda Ibrahim Lodi katika Vita vya Kwanza vya Panipat na kuteka Delhi.
Alishinda muungano wa Rajput katika Vita vya Khanwa, akiimarisha utawala wake.
Alifariki mjini Agra, Milki ya Mughal.

keyAchievements.title: Mwanzilishi wa Milki ya Mughal
keyAchievements.description: Kupitia ushindi wake katika Vita vya Panipat, aliweka msingi wa moja ya milki zenye nguvu zaidi katika historia ya India.

keyAchievements.title: Mwandishi wa Baburnama
keyAchievements.description: Aliandika mojawapo ya tawasifu za kwanza katika fasihi ya Kiislamu, akitoa ufahamu muhimu sana kuhusu maisha yake na nyakati zake.

keyAchievements.title: Mwanzilishi wa Vita vya Kutumia Bunduki India
keyAchievements.description: Utumiaji wake wa kimkakati wa mizinga na silaha za moto ulikuwa sababu kuu ya ushindi wake wa kijeshi nchini India.

faq.question: Jina 'Babur' linamaanisha nini?
faq.answer: Jina 'Babur' linatokana na neno la Kiajemi lenye maana ya 'Simbamarara', ambalo ni jina la utani alilopata kutokana na ushujaa na ujasiri wake vitani.

faq.question: Kwa nini Samarkand ilikuwa muhimu sana kwake?
faq.answer: Samarkand ulikuwa mji mkuu mkuu wa babu yake Timur (Tamerlane), na kuuteka tena kulikuwa lengo lake la maisha, ambalo liliashiria haki yake halali ya kurithi Milki ya Timur.

faq.question: Je, Baburnama ni sahihi kihistoria?
faq.answer: Baburnama inachukuliwa kuwa na uwazi na ukweli wa kushangaza kwa nyakati hizo. Ijapokuwa imeandikwa kwa mtazamo wake, inatoa maelezo mengi sio tu kuhusu ushindi wake, bali pia juu ya kushindwa na udhaifu wake, hivyo kuwa chanzo muhimu sana cha kihistoria.

timeline.event: Born as a 'porphyrogennitos' (born in the purple), a son of Emperor Romanos II and Empress Theophano.
Becomes senior emperor after the death of John I Tzimiskes, but real power is held by the eunuch Basil Lekapenos.
Faces and eventually crushes a series of large-scale internal rebellions led by the powerful generals Bardas Skleros and Bardas Phokas.
Forms a crucial alliance with Vladimir I of Kiev, receiving 6,000 Norse mercenaries (the foundation of the Varangian Guard) in exchange for his sister Anna's hand in marriage.
Achieves a decisive victory over the Bulgarian army at the Battle of Kleidion.
Completes the conquest of the First Bulgarian Empire, annexing it fully into the Byzantine Empire.
Dies while planning a military expedition to reclaim Sicily, leaving the empire at its territorial and financial zenith.

keyAchievements.title: Conquest of Bulgaria
keyAchievements.description: After a brutal, decades-long war, he completely subjugated the First Bulgarian Empire, the empire's main European foe for centuries.

keyAchievements.title: Formation of the Varangian Guard
keyAchievements.description: He established the legendary Varangian Guard, an elite unit of Norse mercenaries who served as the emperor's personal, incorruptible bodyguards.

keyAchievements.title: Fiscal Consolidation
keyAchievements.description: Through austere living, land reforms targeting the powerful aristocracy, and efficient taxation, he left the imperial treasury with a massive surplus upon his death.

keyAchievements.title: Eastern Campaigns
keyAchievements.description: He led successful campaigns against the Fatimid Caliphate and other eastern powers, securing the empire's Anatolian frontier.

faq.question: Why was he really called the 'Bulgar-Slayer'?
faq.answer: He earned the epithet 'Boulgaroktonos' for his relentless and brutal subjugation of the Bulgarian Empire. The name became permanently associated with him after his victory at Kleidion in 1014, following which he allegedly blinded 15,000 Bulgarian prisoners.

faq.question: Did he ever marry or have children?
faq.answer: No. Basil II dedicated his life entirely to the state and the military. He never married and died childless, which ultimately led to a succession crisis and contributed to the decline of the Macedonian dynasty after his death.

faq.question: What was his relationship with the Kievan Rus'?
faq.answer: He formed a pivotal alliance with Vladimir the Great. In exchange for military aid (the Varangian Guard) and Basil's sister Anna in marriage, Vladimir converted to Orthodox Christianity, leading to the Christianization of the entire Kievan Rus'.

faq.question: Was he a good administrator?
faq.answer: Yes, he was a highly effective administrator. He curbed the power of the land-owning aristocracy, reformed the tax system to be more equitable and efficient, and practiced extreme fiscal austerity, leaving the empire incredibly wealthy.

timeline.event: Born in Pingyang, which is modern-day Linfen, Shanxi, China.
Departs from the Eastern Jin capital of Chang'an to travel to India in search of Buddhist scriptures.
Successfully crosses the Taklamakan Desert and the treacherous Pamir Mountains, arriving in Northwest India.
Travels extensively through northern India, studying Sanskrit and collecting scriptures, spending three years in Pataliputra.
Travels to Sri Lanka (the 'Lion Kingdom') to obtain more texts, then begins his perilous sea voyage home.
After surviving severe storms and an unplanned stop in Java, he finally arrives back in China at Laoshan, in modern Shandong.
Resides in Jiankang (modern Nanjing), where he translates the scriptures he collected and dictates his travelogue, 'A Record of Buddhist Kingdoms' (Foguo Ji).
Dies at Xin Monastery in Jingzhou at the approximate age of 85.

keyAchievements.title: Pilgrimage to India
keyAchievements.description: Completed a 14-year overland and sea journey from China to India and back to retrieve Buddhist scriptures at an advanced age.

keyAchievements.title: A Record of Buddhist Kingdoms (Foguo Ji)
keyAchievements.description: Authored one of history's most important travelogues, providing an invaluable eyewitness account of Central and South Asia in the early 5th century.

keyAchievements.title: Translation of Buddhist Texts
keyAchievements.description: Translated key Sanskrit texts into Chinese, most notably the Mahāsāṃghika Vinaya, which significantly influenced monastic discipline in East Asia.

faq.question: Why did Faxian travel to India?
faq.answer: He was distressed by the incomplete and inconsistent state of the Vinaya (monastic rules) available in China and sought to obtain the original, complete versions from India, the birthplace of Buddhism.

faq.question: How long did Faxian's journey take?
faq.answer: His entire pilgrimage, from leaving Chang'an to returning to China, lasted approximately 14-15 years, from around 399 to 414 CE.

faq.question: Is Faxian's travelogue historically accurate?
faq.answer: Yes, 'A Record of Buddhist Kingdoms' (Foguo Ji) is considered a remarkably accurate and valuable primary source for the history, geography, and religious conditions of the regions he visited in the early 5th century.

faq.question: Did Faxian travel alone?
faq.answer: No, he started his journey from Chang'an with a group of other monks, including Huijing, Daozheng, Huiying, and Huiwei. However, due to the extreme hardships of the journey, some died and others chose to stay in India, and Faxian ultimately returned to China alone from his original group.

timeline.event: Born in Germania, a fortified town in what is now modern Bulgaria.
Appointed as a general on the eastern frontier by Emperor Justin I.
Plays a key role in suppressing the Nika riots in Constantinople, saving Emperor Justinian's throne.
Leads the Vandalic War, conquering the Vandal Kingdom in North Africa in less than a year.
Leads the initial and most significant campaigns of the Gothic War to reconquer Italy from the Ostrogoths.
Captures the Ostrogothic capital of Ravenna and their king, Witiges.
In retirement, he is recalled to defend Constantinople from a Kutrigur Bulgar invasion, which he successfully repels.
Accused of conspiracy against Justinian, he is disgraced and briefly imprisoned before being pardoned.
Dies in Constantinople, a few months after his Emperor, Justinian.

keyAchievements.title: Conquest of the Vandal Kingdom
keyAchievements.description: He swiftly destroyed the Vandal Kingdom in North Africa in under a year (533-534), a feat considered a masterpiece of military planning and execution.

keyAchievements.title: Reconquest of Italy
keyAchievements.description: He led the initial, largely successful phase of the Gothic War, capturing Sicily, Naples, Rome, and the Gothic capital of Ravenna against numerically superior forces.

keyAchievements.title: Defense of the East
keyAchievements.description: He fought with distinction against the Sassanian Empire, securing the eastern frontier of the Byzantine Empire at the Battle of Dara.

keyAchievements.title: Suppression of the Nika Riots
keyAchievements.description: His decisive and ruthless action alongside the eunuch Narses crushed the massive urban revolt in 532, saving Justinian's throne from collapse.

faq.question: Was Belisarius really blinded and forced to beg?
faq.answer: This is a popular legend that emerged in the Middle Ages, but there is no contemporary historical evidence to support it. He was briefly disgraced and imprisoned in 562 but his property and freedom were restored by Emperor Justinian.

faq.question: Why was Emperor Justinian so suspicious of him?
faq.answer: Belisarius's immense popularity, stunning military successes, and the fact that the Goths offered him the Western throne made him a potential rival in the eyes of the often-paranoid Justinian and his court.

faq.question: Who was the main historian who wrote about Belisarius?
faq.answer: His personal secretary, Procopius of Caesarea, wrote extensively about his campaigns in 'The Wars of Justinian' and offered a much more critical, scandalous view in the posthumously published 'Secret History'.

timeline.event: Born into a family of minor Ukrainian nobility.
Captured by the Ottomans at the Battle of Cecora and spent two years in captivity.
The "Chyhyryn affair" where Polish official Daniel Czapliński seizes his estate, leading to a personal vendetta that sparks a national rebellion.
Begins the Khmelnytsky Uprising, securing major victories at Zhovti Vody and Korsun.
Signs the Treaty of Zboriv, which recognizes the Cossack Hetmanate as an autonomous entity within the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth.
Suffers a major defeat at the Battle of Berestechko after being abandoned by his Tatar allies.
Signs the Treaty of Pereiaslav, placing the Cossack Hetmanate under the protection of the Tsar of Muscovy.
Dies in Chyhyryn, leaving behind a nascent but unstable state.

keyAchievements.title: Leadership of the Khmelnytsky Uprising
keyAchievements.description: Successfully organized and led a massive popular rebellion against the powerful Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth.

keyAchievements.title: Establishment of the Cossack Hetmanate
keyAchievements.description: Created the first semi-independent Ukrainian Cossack state, which served as a foundation for modern Ukrainian national identity.

keyAchievements.title: Military Victories
keyAchievements.description: Achieved a series of stunning military victories against Polish forces, demonstrating brilliant tactical and strategic leadership.

faq.question: Was Khmelnytsky a hero or a villain?
faq.answer: He is a deeply controversial figure. To many Ukrainians, he is a national hero who founded their first modern state. To Poles and Jews, his legacy is far more negative due to the immense violence and massacres committed during his uprising.

faq.question: What was the significance of the Treaty of Pereiaslav?
faq.answer: It was a pivotal moment. Seeking protection from Poland, Khmelnytsky allied with the Tsardom of Muscovy. This act is seen by some as a necessary evil for survival, and by others as the moment Ukraine traded Polish domination for Russian domination, leading to centuries of Russian influence.

faq.question: Why did the uprising involve such widespread violence against Jews?
faq.answer: The Jewish population was often associated with the Polish szlachta (nobility) as leaseholders and tax collectors (arendators), making them targets of the rebels' fury against the socio-economic system. The uprising unleashed long-simmering resentments, resulting in horrific massacres.

timeline.event: Emerged as chieftain of the Ashina Turks, vassals of the Rouran Khaganate.
Suppressed a Tiele revolt for the Rouran and requested a Rouran princess in marriage as a reward.
The Rouran Khagan Anagui refused and insulted Bumin, sparking the Turkic rebellion.
Allied with the Western Wei, married Princess Changle, and defeated the Rouran forces. Rouran Khagan Anagui committed suicide.
Declared himself Ilig Qaghan at the Otuken capital, founding the First Turkic Khaganate.
Died shortly after founding the Khaganate. His brother Istämi was appointed Yabgu (ruler) of the western territories.

keyAchievements.title: Revolt Against the Rouran
keyAchievements.description: Led a successful rebellion against the Rouran Khaganate, his people's long-time overlords.

keyAchievements.title: Founding the Turkic Khaganate
keyAchievements.description: Established the First Turkic Khaganate in 552, initiating a new era of Turkic power in Eurasia.

keyAchievements.title: Strategic Alliance with Western Wei
keyAchievements.description: Secured a crucial alliance with the Chinese Western Wei dynasty, which legitimized his rule and provided military support.

faq.question: Who were the Göktürks?
faq.answer: The Göktürks (or 'Celestial Turks') were a confederation of Turkic peoples in medieval Inner Asia, founded by Bumin Qaghan. They were the first Turkic state to use the name 'Türk' in a political context.

faq.question: What does 'Qaghan' mean?
faq.answer: 'Qaghan' (or Khagan) is a title of imperial rank in Turkic, Mongol, and other nomadic languages, equivalent to the status of an emperor.

faq.question: Why was being a blacksmith significant?
faq.answer: For steppe nomads, blacksmiths were highly respected and even feared, believed to hold special powers over fire and metal. The Rouran Khagan's insult, calling Bumin a 'blacksmith slave', was therefore a deep social and cultural affront.

timeline.event: Born, the illegitimate son of Sviatoslav I of Kyiv.
Defeats and kills his half-brother Yaropolk, becoming Grand Prince of Kyiv.
Is baptized into Orthodox Christianity in Chersonesus, marries Byzantine princess Anna Porphyrogenita.
Organizes the mass baptism of the people of Kyiv in the Dnieper River, initiating the Christianization of Rus'.
Dies at Berestove, near Kyiv, after ruling for 35 years.

keyAchievements.title: Unification of Kievan Rus'
keyAchievements.description: He consolidated the lands of the Rus' from the Baltic to the Black Sea, unifying various East Slavic tribes under his rule.

keyAchievements.title: Christianization of Kievan Rus'
keyAchievements.description: His adoption of Orthodox Christianity in 988 as the state religion became a pivotal moment in Eastern European history.

keyAchievements.title: Cultural and Legal Reforms
keyAchievements.description: Following his conversion, he established schools, churches, and ecclesiastical courts, laying the foundation for a new Christian society.

faq.question: Why is he called 'the Great'?
faq.answer: He is called 'the Great' for unifying the lands of Kievan Rus' and for his monumental decision to Christianize the realm, which defined its cultural and religious identity for centuries.

faq.question: Was Vladimir a saint?
faq.answer: Yes, despite his brutal early life as a pagan, his later piety and work in establishing Christianity led to his canonization as a saint in the Orthodox Church, where he is known as 'Saint Vladimir the Equal-to-the-Apostles'.

faq.question: Why did he choose Orthodoxy over other religions?
faq.answer: His envoys were awestruck by the beauty of the liturgy at Hagia Sophia in Constantinople. This, combined with the strategic advantage of a military and marital alliance with the powerful Byzantine Empire, made Orthodoxy the most appealing choice.

timeline.event: Born in Saint Petersburg, Russian Empire.
Graduates from Saint Petersburg State University.
Becomes a full member of the St. Petersburg Academy of Sciences.
Founds and becomes the first president of the Ukrainian Academy of Sciences in Kyiv.
Becomes director of the newly founded Radium Institute in Petrograd.
Publishes his seminal work, "The Biosphere," in Russian (French translation in 1929).
Publishes his final paper on the noosphere.
Dies in Moscow, Soviet Union.

keyAchievements.title: Concept of the Biosphere
keyAchievements.description: He redefined the biosphere as the global ecological system integrating all living beings and their relationships, including their interaction with the elements of the lithosphere, hydrosphere, and atmosphere.

keyAchievements.title: Founding Biogeochemistry
keyAchievements.description: He established biogeochemistry as a new scientific discipline, studying the chemical processes and cycles that are driven by or impact biological activity.

keyAchievements.title: Concept of the Noosphere
keyAchievements.description: He theorized the noosphere as the third stage in Earth's development, after the geosphere and biosphere, representing the sphere of human reason and consciousness as a geological force.

keyAchievements.title: Ukrainian Academy of Sciences
keyAchievements.description: He was a principal founder and the first president of the Ukrainian Academy of Sciences, establishing a major center for scientific research in Kyiv.

faq.question: What is the difference between the biosphere and the noosphere?
faq.answer: The biosphere is the global sum of all ecosystems, the zone of life on Earth. The noosphere is Vernadsky's concept for the next evolutionary stage, where human reason and consciousness become the dominant force shaping the biosphere.

faq.question: How is Vernadsky's work related to the Gaia hypothesis?
faq.answer: Vernadsky's concept of the biosphere as a geological force that transforms the planet predates and influenced James Lovelock's Gaia hypothesis, which similarly views the Earth as a self-regulating system. However, Vernadsky's focus was more on the chemical and physical impact of life, whereas Gaia includes the idea of homeostatic feedback loops.

faq.question: Was Vernadsky a Russian or Ukrainian scientist?
faq.answer: He is claimed by both nations. He was born in the Russian Empire to a family with Ukrainian Cossack roots, identified with both Russian and Ukrainian cultures, and was a foundational figure for both the Russian and Ukrainian Academies of Sciences.

timeline.event: Born in Zhytomyr, Russian Empire (now Ukraine).
Co-founds the Group for the Study of Reactive Motion (GIRD), a key Soviet rocketry research group.
Arrested during Stalin's Great Purge and sentenced to 10 years in a labor camp.
Released from prison by a special decree, though his conviction was not cleared until 1957.
As Chief Designer, he oversees the successful launch of Sputnik 1, the world's first artificial satellite.
Oversees the Vostok 1 mission, which makes Yuri Gagarin the first human to orbit the Earth.
Dies during surgery. His identity as the 'Chief Designer' is finally revealed to the public, and he is buried with state honors in the Kremlin Wall Necropolis.

keyAchievements.title: R-7 Semyorka Rocket
keyAchievements.description: Designed the world's first intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM), which was adapted into the launch vehicle for Sputnik and Vostok.

keyAchievements.title: Sputnik Program
keyAchievements.description: Led the program that launched Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite, initiating the Space Age and the Space Race.

keyAchievements.title: Vostok Program
keyAchievements.description: Masterminded the Vostok program, which successfully sent Yuri Gagarin into orbit as the first human in space.

keyAchievements.title: First Lunar and Planetary Probes
keyAchievements.description: Oversaw the Luna program, which achieved the first impact on the Moon (Luna 2) and first photos of its far side (Luna 3).

faq.question: Why was Sergei Korolev's identity kept secret?
faq.answer: His role as 'Chief Designer' was a state secret to protect him from potential foreign assassination attempts and to present Soviet achievements as the work of the collective people, rather than one individual.

faq.question: What was the cause of his death?
faq.answer: He died from heart failure during what was supposed to be a routine operation to remove a polyp. His health had been severely and permanently damaged by his years in the Gulag.

faq.question: How did he get out of the Kolyma Gulag?
faq.answer: Fellow aviation designer Andrei Tupolev, himself a political prisoner in a special prison (sharashka), personally intervened and requested Korolev's transfer to his design bureau to work on military aircraft.

faq.question: What is his legacy?
faq.answer: Korolev is considered the father of practical astronautics. His vision and relentless drive were the primary forces behind the early Soviet space successes that defined the beginning of the Space Age.

timeline.event: Born in Veliky Ustyug, Russia.
Recruited for Siberian service as a Cossack.
Moves to the Yakutsk ostrog (fort).
Embarks on the epic sea voyage from the Kolyma River, sailing around the Chukchi Peninsula.
After being shipwrecked, reaches the Anadyr River and establishes the Anadyrsk ostrog.
Discovers a major walrus rookery, a key source of valuable ivory.
Travels overland from Yakutsk to Moscow to deliver tribute, a journey that takes over a year.
Dies in Moscow while on official business.
German historian Gerhardt Friedrich Müller discovers Dezhnev's reports in the Yakutsk archives, finally bringing his achievement to light.

keyAchievements.title: First European Circumnavigation of the Chukchi Peninsula
keyAchievements.description: He became the first recorded European to sail through the strait separating Asia and North America, now known as the Bering Strait.

keyAchievements.title: Proving Asia and North America are Separate
keyAchievements.description: His voyage provided the first concrete evidence that the two continents were not connected by a land bridge.

keyAchievements.title: Exploration of Northeastern Siberia
keyAchievements.description: He founded the Anadyrsk ostrog, the first Russian settlement in Chukotka, and explored the Anadyr River basin.

faq.question: Why is it called the Bering Strait and not the Dezhnev Strait?
faq.answer: Dezhnev's detailed report of his voyage was filed away in government archives in Yakutsk and was not widely known. It was only rediscovered in 1736, years after the Danish explorer Vitus Bering, serving the Russian Navy, had sailed the same waters in 1728 and had the strait named after him.

faq.question: What was the primary goal of Dezhnev's 1648 expedition?
faq.answer: The main purpose was economic, not purely scientific. The expedition sought valuable resources, particularly walrus ivory ('fish teeth') and sable fur, which were highly prized commodities for trade.

faq.question: What happened to Dezhnev after his famous voyage?
faq.answer: He spent over a decade in the Anadyr region, collecting tribute and exploring. He later served in Yakutsk and made a remarkable overland journey to Moscow to deliver a massive collection of tribute. He died in Moscow in 1673, a respected but not widely famous Cossack ataman.

timeline.event: Believed to have been born in the Rustavi region of Georgia.
Active during the reign of Queen Tamar, serving in her court, possibly as the Royal Treasurer (Mechurchletukhutsesi).
Composed his magnum opus, 'The Knight in the Panther's Skin'.
According to legend, he retired to the Georgian Monastery of the Cross in Jerusalem.
Estimated year of death, possibly in Jerusalem.

keyAchievements.title: The Knight in the Panther's Skin (ვეფხისტყაოსანი)
keyAchievements.description: Authored Georgia's national epic poem, a masterpiece of medieval literature celebrating love, friendship, and chivalry.

keyAchievements.title: Pinnacle of the Georgian Golden Age
keyAchievements.description: His work is considered the highest literary achievement of Georgia's most prosperous cultural and political era.

keyAchievements.title: Foundation of Modern Georgian Language
keyAchievements.description: His poem played a crucial role in shaping and standardizing the modern Georgian literary language.

faq.question: Is 'The Knight in the Panther's Skin' a true story?
faq.answer: It is a work of fiction, an epic poem that blends romance, adventure, and philosophical ideals, not a historical account.

faq.question: What was his relationship with Queen Tamar?
faq.answer: He served in her court, possibly as Royal Treasurer. Legends suggest he was secretly in love with her and dedicated his epic to her, but this is not historically proven.

faq.question: Why is so little known about his life?
faq.answer: Few contemporary records from 12th-century Georgia survive, and those that do focus more on royalty and military events than on poets, even famous ones.

faq.question: Where can one see the supposed portrait of Rustaveli?
faq.answer: A fresco on a pillar in the Monastery of the Cross in Jerusalem is traditionally believed to be a portrait of Rustaveli. The original was defaced, but a copy remains.

timeline.event: Born in Thessaly, Byzantine Empire.
Moved to Constantinople to teach privately after studying on the island of Andros.
Appointed Archbishop of Thessalonica by Emperor Theophilos.
Deposed as Archbishop following the official end of Byzantine Iconoclasm.
His fame reaches the Abbasid Caliph, who invites him to Baghdad. Leo refuses, gaining favor with the Byzantine Emperor.
Appointed head (scholarch) of the newly re-founded Magnaura School by the regent Bardas.
Last known historical mention of him. His exact death date is unknown.

keyAchievements.title: Revival of Classical Learning
keyAchievements.description: He systematically collected and copied ancient Greek manuscripts, preserving works of mathematics, philosophy, and science.

keyAchievements.title: Leadership of the Magnaura School
keyAchievements.description: As head of the imperial university, he educated a new generation of scholars, sparking the Macedonian Renaissance.

keyAchievements.title: Invention of a Communication System
keyAchievements.description: He is credited with inventing a system of beacons, an optical telegraph, that stretched across Anatolia to warn of Arab raids.

faq.question: Why is he called 'the Mathematician'?
faq.answer: While a polymath, his exceptional skill in mathematics and logic was his most defining trait, earning him the title. His work involved algebra and the study of classical Greek mathematicians.

faq.question: Did he really turn down an offer from the Caliph?
faq.answer: Yes, historical accounts state that the Abbasid Caliph, impressed by his knowledge, offered him immense wealth to come to Baghdad. Leo, encouraged by the Byzantine Emperor, chose to remain in Constantinople.

faq.question: What was the Macedonian Renaissance?
faq.answer: It was a 9th and 10th-century cultural revival in the Byzantine Empire, marked by a renewed interest in classical Greek scholarship, art, and literature. Leo was a central figure in this movement.

timeline.event: Born as Ibrahim Qunanbaiuly in the Chingis Mountains, modern-day Kazakhstan.
Attended a madrasa in Semipalatinsk, followed by a Russian school, giving him a unique dual education.
Began to write poetry with serious dedication, adopting the pen name 'Abai'.
Wrote his major philosophical work, the 'Book of Words' (Kara Sozder).
Suffered the death of his son, Abdirakhman.
Died in the Zhidebay valley, shortly after the death of his other son, Magauiya.

keyAchievements.title: Founder of Modern Kazakh Literature
keyAchievements.description: He reformed the Kazakh literary language, introducing new poetic forms, genres, and themes, effectively creating its modern written form.

keyAchievements.title: The 'Book of Words' (Kara Sozder)
keyAchievements.description: A collection of 45 philosophical prose pieces and parables critiquing Kazakh society and advocating for education, morality, and reform.

keyAchievements.title: Cultural Bridge
keyAchievements.description: He translated works by Russian and European authors like Pushkin, Lermontov, and Goethe, introducing new ideas to the Kazakh people.

keyAchievements.title: Composer
keyAchievements.description: He composed over two dozen melodies for his own poems, which have become an integral part of Kazakh folk music.

faq.question: Why is he called Abai?
faq.answer: 'Abai' (meaning 'careful' or 'attentive') was a nickname given to him by his grandmother, Zere, which he later adopted as his pen name. His birth name was Ibrahim.

faq.question: What are the 'Words of Edification'?
faq.answer: Also known as the 'Book of Words' (Kara Sozder), it is his magnum opus, a collection of 45 philosophical reflections on Kazakh culture, morality, law, and the future of his people.

faq.question: Was Abai only a writer?
faq.answer: No, Abai was also a gifted composer. He composed over two dozen melodies, many of which set his own poems to music and have become classic Kazakh folk songs.

faq.question: What was his view on Russia?
faq.answer: Abai had a complex view. He was critical of Tsarist colonial policy but admired Russian literature and culture, seeing it as a source of enlightenment and progress that could benefit the Kazakh people if approached selectively.

timeline.event: Born in the village of Grigorovo, Nizhny Novgorod region, into a priestly family.
Begins his public opposition to the liturgical reforms initiated by Patriarch Nikon of Moscow.
Arrested and exiled to Siberia with his family. Endures a decade of extreme hardship and travel.
Briefly returns to Moscow but refuses to compromise, leading to his re-arrest and exile to the Russian North.
Defrocked and anathematized by the Great Moscow Synod. Imprisoned in an underground pit in Pustozersk.
Writes his autobiography, 'The Life of Avvakum, Written by Himself', a foundational work of Russian literature.
Burned at the stake in Pustozersk along with his closest followers for 'great blasphemies against the Tsar'.

keyAchievements.title: Leader of the Old Believers
keyAchievements.description: Became the most prominent and unyielding leader of the Raskol (schism), galvanizing opposition to the state-backed church reforms.

keyAchievements.title: Author of 'The Life of Avvakum'
keyAchievements.description: Wrote the first major Russian autobiography, a masterpiece of 17th-century literature known for its vivid, colloquial language and passionate defense of his faith.

faq.question: What were the church reforms Avvakum opposed?
faq.answer: Patriarch Nikon's reforms aimed to align Russian Orthodox practices with the Greek Orthodox Church. This included changing the sign of the cross from two fingers to three, altering the spelling of Jesus's name ('Isus' to 'Iisus'), and revising liturgical books. Avvakum and the Old Believers saw these changes as heresy.

faq.question: Why is his autobiography so important?
faq.answer: It was one of the first major works written in colloquial, everyday Russian rather than the formal Church Slavonic. Its raw, personal, and passionate style broke new ground in Russian literature and provided a powerful testament to the Old Believer cause.

faq.question: Was Avvakum a saint?
faq.answer: He is not recognized as a saint by the mainstream Russian Orthodox Church. However, he is venerated as a holy martyr and saint by many denominations of the Old Believers, who see him as the ultimate defender of the true faith.

timeline.event: Becomes co-ruler of the Huns with his brother Bleda.
Murders Bleda to become the sole ruler of the Hunnic Empire.
Launches a major invasion of the Balkans and threatens Constantinople.
Invades Gaul and fights the Battle of the Catalaunian Plains.
Invades Italy, sacks several cities, but spares Rome.
Dies on his wedding night; his empire collapses shortly after.

keyAchievements.title: Unification of the Hunnic Tribes
keyAchievements.description: He united various Hunnic and Germanic tribes into a formidable, centralized fighting force under his sole command.

keyAchievements.title: Subjugation of the Eastern Roman Empire
keyAchievements.description: He repeatedly defeated the Eastern Roman armies, forcing them to pay enormous annual tributes in gold.

keyAchievements.title: Creation of a Vast European Empire
keyAchievements.description: He established a massive, though short-lived, empire stretching from the Ural River to the Rhine and from the Danube to the Baltic Sea.

faq.question: Why is he called the 'Scourge of God'?
faq.answer: Christian writers in Europe gave him this name, believing his devastating campaigns were a divine punishment for their sins.

faq.question: Why didn't he conquer Rome in 452?
faq.answer: Historians debate this. Reasons may include a plea from Pope Leo I, disease and famine in his army, or the threat of an Eastern Roman attack on his homeland.

faq.question: How did Attila die?
faq.answer: He died in 453 on the night of his wedding to a new bride named Ildico. The most accepted cause is a severe nosebleed or internal bleeding, possibly exacerbated by heavy drinking.

timeline.event: Born in Sayram, now in Kazakhstan.
Travels to Bukhara to study under the renowned Sufi master Yusuf Hamadani.
Briefly becomes the head of Hamadani's khaneqah (Sufi lodge) before returning to his hometown of Yasi.
At age 63, in mourning for the Prophet Muhammad, he retreats to an underground cell (chilla-khana) to live out his life in contemplation.
Dies in the city of Yasi (present-day Turkistan, Kazakhstan), where his mausoleum now stands.

keyAchievements.title: Diwan-i-Hikmet (Book of Wisdom)
keyAchievements.description: Authored a collection of poems in the Turkic language, making complex Sufi philosophy accessible to the common people of Central Asia.

keyAchievements.title: Founder of the Yasawiyya Order
keyAchievements.description: Established the first major Turkic Sufi order, which spread his teachings widely across the Turkic-speaking world for centuries.

keyAchievements.title: Pioneer of Turkic Religious Literature
keyAchievements.description: Elevated the status of the Turkic language as a vehicle for profound religious and mystical expression, influencing centuries of literature.

faq.question: Why is Ahmad Yasawi so important?
faq.answer: He was the first major mystic to compose poetry in a Turkic language, which allowed Islamic and Sufi ideas to spread widely among the nomadic and settled Turkic peoples of Central Asia.

faq.question: What is the 'Diwan-i-Hikmet'?
faq.answer: It is his collection of poems, called 'hikmats' (wisdoms), which explain the principles of Sufism, ethics, and love for God in a simple, accessible style using Turkic folk-poetry meters.

faq.question: Why did he go underground at age 63?
faq.answer: He did so out of profound respect and mourning for the Prophet Muhammad, who died at the age of 63. Yasawi felt it was improper for him to continue living above ground and see a sun the Prophet could no longer see.

timeline.event: Born in the Porphyra chamber of the Great Palace of Constantinople, as the first child of Emperor Alexios I Komnenos and Irene Doukaina.
As a teenager, she witnesses the arrival of the leaders of the First Crusade in Constantinople, an event she would later chronicle in detail.

Her father, Alexios I, dies. She and her mother attempt to persuade him to name her husband, Nikephoros Bryennios, as his successor, but fail. She then conspires against her brother, the new Emperor John II.
After her plot is discovered, she is stripped of her property and exiled to the Kecharitomene Monastery, which her mother had founded.
Following the death of her husband, she begins writing the Alexiad, a 15-book history of her father's reign, likely completing a work her husband had started.
Dies in the monastery, having completed her monumental historical work.

keyAchievements.title: Author of the Alexiad
keyAchievements.description: She wrote the Alexiad, a 15-book historical text that is one of the most important primary sources for the history of the Byzantine Empire and the First Crusade.

keyAchievements.title: Pioneering Female Historian
keyAchievements.description: She is widely regarded as one of the first and most significant female historians in the Western world, setting a precedent for female scholarship.

keyAchievements.title: Medical Administrator and Scholar
keyAchievements.description: She possessed extensive medical knowledge, administered a large hospital and orphanage in Constantinople, and wrote about diseases and treatments in the Alexiad.

faq.question: Was Anna Komnene really supposed to be Empress?
faq.answer: As the firstborn child, she was initially the designated heir and was betrothed to the co-emperor, placing her in the direct line of succession. Her status changed after her brother John was born and later designated as heir by their father.

faq.question: What is the Alexiad about?
faq.answer: It is a detailed account of the political and military history of the Byzantine Empire during the reign of her father, Alexios I Komnenos, from 1081 to 1118. It also provides a unique Byzantine perspective on the First Crusade.

faq.question: Is the Alexiad a reliable historical source?
faq.answer: While it is an invaluable firsthand account, it is also highly biased. Anna glorifies her father and family while often criticizing or downplaying their rivals. Historians use it critically, cross-referencing it with Latin, Armenian, and other sources to get a more balanced view.

timeline.event: Born, likely in the Principality of Moscow, during the era of the Tatar Yoke.
Collaborated with Theophanes the Greek and Prokhor of Gorodets on the frescoes for the Cathedral of the Annunciation in the Moscow Kremlin.
Painted frescoes and the iconostasis in the Dormition Cathedral in Vladimir, alongside his friend and collaborator Daniil Chyorny.
